what_it_solves: continuous outcome prediction from linear relationships
when_to_use: interpretable baseline, continuous target, linear feature-target relationship
when_not_to_use: nonlinear relationships, high-dimensional sparse data, classification

what_it_solves: binary/multiclass classification with probability output
when_to_use: binary outcome, need probability estimates, interpretability required
when_not_to_use: nonlinear decision boundaries, very high-dimensional features without regularization

what_it_solves: binary outcome modelled as linear function of predictors
when_to_use: quick interpretable baseline for binary outcomes
when_not_to_use: need bounded probabilities, production deployment

what_it_solves: flexible nonlinear regression without parametric assumptions
when_to_use: nonlinear relationships, sufficient data density
when_not_to_use: high dimensions (curse of dimensionality), small datasets

what_it_solves: piecewise polynomial regression with knot-based flexibility
when_to_use: need smooth nonlinear fit with controlled flexibility
when_not_to_use: simple linear relationships, very small data
governance_risks: overfitting with too many degrees of freedom

what_it_solves: probabilistic classification using Bayes theorem with independence assumption
when_to_use: text classification, high-dimensional sparse data, fast baseline
when_not_to_use: features are strongly correlated, need precise probability calibration

what_it_solves: classification and regression by local averaging of nearest training points
when_to_use: nonlinear boundaries, low-dimensional data, interpretable local predictions
when_not_to_use: high dimensions (curse of dimensionality), large datasets (slow inference), irrelevant features
failure_modes: k too small → overfit, k too large → underfit

what_it_solves: systematic hyperparameter tuning with out-of-sample validation
when_to_use: any model with hyperparameters, need reliable generalization estimate
when_not_to_use: computationally prohibitive search space without pruning
linked_problems: hyperparameter optimization for any ML model
governance_risks: data leakage if validation not properly isolated
failure_modes: grid too coarse misses optimal region

what_it_solves: model stability estimation and hyperparameter tuning via sampling with replacement
when_to_use: small datasets, need confidence in model stability, OOB error estimation
when_not_to_use: very large datasets where k-fold is cheaper
governance_risks: ~36.8% OOB may not represent full data distribution

what_it_solves: partition data into K distinct groups by centroid proximity
when_to_use: spherical clusters, known K, large datasets
when_not_to_use: non-spherical clusters, unknown K, outlier-heavy data

what_it_solves: density-based clustering that discovers arbitrary-shape clusters and identifies noise
when_to_use: unknown number of clusters, arbitrary shapes, need outlier detection
when_not_to_use: varying density clusters, very high dimensions

what_it_solves: nested cluster hierarchy via agglomerative or divisive merging
when_to_use: want dendrogram visualization, don't know K, small-medium datasets
when_not_to_use: large datasets (O(n^3) complexity)

what_it_solves: classification and regression via recursive binary splitting
when_to_use: interpretability required, mixed feature types, feature importance needed
when_not_to_use: need high accuracy (use ensembles), extrapolation required

what_it_solves: variance reduction by averaging predictions from bootstrap-aggregated models
when_to_use: high-variance base learners (trees), want stability
when_not_to_use: base learner already low variance, need interpretability

what_it_solves: classification and regression via decorrelated bagged trees
when_to_use: general-purpose prediction, feature importance, robust baseline
when_not_to_use: need real-time inference on constrained hardware, need full interpretability
governance_risks: feature importance can be misleading with correlated features

what_it_solves: sequential bias reduction by fitting residuals of previous models
when_to_use: need high accuracy, structured/tabular data
when_not_to_use: small datasets (overfitting risk), need fast training
failure_modes: learning rate too high → overfit, too low → slow

what_it_solves: adaptive boosting with sample reweighting
when_to_use: classification with weak learners
when_not_to_use: noisy data (amplifies noise through reweighting)

what_it_solves: optimized gradient boosting with regularization and parallel processing
when_to_use: competition-grade accuracy on tabular data, need speed + regularization
when_not_to_use: simple problems where interpretability matters more

what_it_solves: optimal separating hyperplane for classification, with kernel trick for nonlinear boundaries
when_to_use: binary classification, small-medium datasets, high-dimensional space
when_not_to_use: very large datasets, need probability output, need interpretability

what_it_solves: learn complex nonlinear representations from data
when_to_use: large datasets, complex patterns, image/text/sequence data
when_not_to_use: small data, need interpretability, tabular data (often RF/XGBoost better)

what_it_solves: spatial pattern recognition in images and structured data
when_to_use: image classification, object detection, spatial feature extraction
when_not_to_use: sequential data (use RNN/LSTM), tabular data

what_it_solves: sequential pattern recognition with temporal dependencies
when_to_use: time series, text sequences, speech
when_not_to_use: non-sequential data, very long sequences without attention

what_it_solves: learn dense vector representations of words from context
when_to_use: need word embeddings for downstream ML, similarity tasks
when_not_to_use: need contextual embeddings (use transformers)

what_it_solves: context-aware sequence processing with self-attention mechanism
when_to_use: text understanding/generation, machine translation, any sequence task
when_not_to_use: very small data, computational constraints

what_it_solves: adapt pretrained transformer models to specific downstream tasks
when_to_use: domain-specific NLP task with limited labeled data
when_not_to_use: pretrained model already performs well zero-shot

what_it_solves: maximize/minimize linear objective subject to linear constraints
when_to_use: resource allocation, production planning, portfolio optimization with linear relationships
when_not_to_use: nonlinear objectives/constraints, integer requirements

what_it_solves: optimization with discrete/binary decision variables
when_to_use: go/no-go decisions, project selection, assignment problems
when_not_to_use: continuous decisions only, very large problems without decomposition

what_it_solves: economic interpretation of constraints and resource valuation
when_to_use: need shadow prices, resource buyout analysis, constraint sensitivity
when_not_to_use: just need optimal solution without economic insight
governance_risks: dual interpretation valid only at optimum
failure_modes: misinterpreting shadow prices outside allowable range

what_it_solves: optimal decisions under uncertainty with expected value calculations
when_to_use: discrete outcomes, known probabilities, sequential decisions
when_not_to_use: continuous uncertainty distributions (use simulation), unknown probabilities

what_it_solves: uncertainty quantification through repeated random sampling
when_to_use: financial modelling under uncertainty, risk analysis, NPV distributions
when_not_to_use: deterministic problems, insufficient distributional knowledge

what_it_solves: multi-step LLM-driven task execution with tool use
when_to_use: business analysis automation, decision support, multi-step reasoning
when_not_to_use: simple single-query tasks, no tool integration needed

what_it_solves: stateful, graph-based multi-agent workflow coordination with conditional routing
when_to_use: complex multi-agent systems, need interrupt/checkpoint control, stateful workflows
when_not_to_use: simple single-agent chains

what_it_solves: human oversight and intervention points in autonomous AI systems
when_to_use: high-stakes decisions, deployment risk, regulatory requirements
when_not_to_use: fully automated low-risk tasks

what_it_solves: comprehensive company valuation and investment recommendation
when_to_use: investment analysis, company due diligence, portfolio decisions
when_not_to_use: short-term trading (use technical analysis)

what_it_solves: sequential decision problems via recursive decomposition using Bellman's principle of optimality
when_to_use: multi-stage decisions, knapsack allocation, resource allocation across time periods
when_not_to_use: non-sequential problems, large continuous state spaces (curse of dimensionality)
exam_formula: f*(s) = max/min over decisions { immediate reward + f*(next state) }

what_it_solves: steady-state probability distribution of systems that transition between states at discrete time steps
when_to_use: system state prediction over time, long-run behaviour analysis, customer retention modelling
when_not_to_use: continuous-time processes (use CTMC), non-Markovian history-dependent systems
governance_risks: Markov property assumption (memoryless) may not hold
failure_modes: non-ergodic chains have no unique steady-state
exam_formula: Solve pi*P = pi with sum(pi) = 1; rows of P sum to 1

what_it_solves: steady-state distribution of systems where transitions occur at continuous rates (arrival/service processes)
when_to_use: queueing systems, call centres, hospital patient flow, server utilization
when_not_to_use: discrete-time state transitions (use DTMC)
governance_risks: steady-state only valid if system is stable (arrival < capacity)
failure_modes: generator matrix rows not summing to 0
exam_formula: Solve pi*Q = 0 with sum(pi) = 1; rows of Q sum to 0

what_it_solves: optimal selection of which facilities to open and which customers to assign to each facility
when_to_use: warehouse/distribution network design, clinic placement, retail site selection
when_not_to_use: single-facility problems (use simple LP), when travel costs dominate and require network flow
governance_risks: equity constraints needed for public-sector facilities
NP-hard — solver may time out on large instances
failure_modes: ignoring fixed vs variable cost split
exam_note: Key constraint: x_ij <= y_j (can only assign customer i to facility j if facility j is open)

what_it_solves: updating prior probabilities with new evidence to compute posterior probabilities for decision trees
when_to_use: decision trees with market research, medical test results, indicator signals that update state probabilities
when_not_to_use: when prior probabilities are unknown or evidence is unreliable
governance_risks: posterior probabilities must sum to 1.0 — immediate recheck if not
failure_modes: arithmetic errors in joint probability table
failing to check posterior sums to 1
exam_formula: Joint = Prior × Likelihood; Marginal = sum of Joints; Posterior = Joint / Marginal

what_it_solves: monetary value of imperfect information from surveys, pilots, or tests — quantifies whether research spend is justified
when_to_use: deciding whether to commission market research, clinical trials, or pilot programs before full investment
when_not_to_use: when information is free or already perfect (use EVPI instead)
governance_risks: sunk cost fallacy if research < EVSI
exam_formula: EVSI = EMV(with research) − EMV(without research) − research cost; Efficiency = EVSI/EVPI

what_it_solves: identify the correct probability distribution from visual or summary statistics for financial modelling and @RISK inputs
when_to_use: setting up Monte Carlo simulations, exam distribution questions, @RISK distribution assignment
when_not_to_use: when empirical data is rich enough to fit directly (use kernel density or empirical CDF)
governance_risks: assuming normality when data is skewed
failure_modes: confusing lognormal with normal for asset returns
applying uniform when data is clustered
ignoring bounded distributions for bounded outcomes
exam_rules: Uniform=flat rectangle bounded; Triangular=tent with min/mode/max; Normal=bell symmetric unbounded; Lognormal=right-skewed bounded at zero

what_it_solves: compute historical or expected returns using arithmetic or geometric mean — choosing correctly matters for compounding
when_to_use: performance reporting, historical analysis, input to financial models, exam return calculations
when_not_to_use: short horizons with no compounding where arithmetic suffices
governance_risks: arithmetic overstates compounded growth when returns are volatile (volatility drag)
using wrong mean type in valuation inputs
failure_modes: reporting geometric when stakeholders expect period return
ignoring volatility drag in long-horizon models
exam_formula: Arithmetic = (sum of returns)/n; Geometric = (product of (1+r_i))^(1/n) - 1; Geometric < Arithmetic when variance > 0

what_it_solves: statistical analysis combining cross-sectional and time-series dimensions to control for unobserved heterogeneity
when_to_use: repeated observations of same entities over time (firms, countries, patients), need to control for entity-level fixed effects
when_not_to_use: pure cross-section or pure time series without repeated observations
governance_risks: model choice (FE vs RE) affects inference — Hausman test required
standard error clustering for serial correlation
exam_note: Identify panel structure → test normality → choose FE/RE → run Hausman test → interpret with business meaning

what_it_solves: separate time series into trend, seasonal, cyclical, and irregular components for forecasting and stationarity testing
when_to_use: seasonal business data, financial time series before modelling, stationarity diagnostics before ARIMA/ML
when_not_to_use: non-temporal cross-sectional data, very short series (< 2 seasonal cycles)
governance_risks: differencing removes real trends not just unit roots
exam_steps: Plot series → identify trend/season → first-difference for stationarity → add dummy variables for seasonality

what_it_solves: flexible non-parametric smoothing using locally weighted polynomial fits — no global functional form assumed
when_to_use: exploratory nonlinear trend visualization, span parameter tuning, flexible baseline before parametric models
when_not_to_use: need extrapolation (LOESS is interpolation only), very large datasets (O(n²) computation)
governance_risks: poor extrapolation beyond training data range
course_note: Evidenced in R scripts CV_loess_1-3.R from MBAN 5560 (Dr. Aydede)

what_it_solves: iterative first-order optimization algorithm for minimizing differentiable loss functions in ML model training
when_to_use: neural network training, logistic regression, any differentiable loss minimization
when_not_to_use: non-differentiable objectives, when closed-form solution exists (use normal equations for linear regression)
linked_problems: any ML model with learnable parameters
governance_risks: learning rate too low → slow convergence
failure_modes: stopping too early or too late
variants: Batch GD, Stochastic GD (SGD), Mini-batch GD, Adam, RMSprop

what_it_solves: quantify each feature's contribution to a model's prediction using game-theoretic Shapley values — model-agnostic explanation
when_to_use: explaining black-box model predictions, feature importance for compliance/audit, understanding individual predictions
when_not_to_use: simple interpretable models where coefficients already explain (use regression output directly)
governance_risks: SHAP is post-hoc explanation not causal claim
correlated features produce unstable SHAP values

what_it_solves: measure feature importance by randomly shuffling each feature and measuring accuracy drop — model-agnostic
when_to_use: any black-box or glass-box model, when built-in feature importance is unavailable or biased
when_not_to_use: highly correlated features (shuffling one may not degrade performance if another compensates)
governance_risks: correlated features cause unstable importance scores
failure_modes: applying on training instead of test set

what_it_solves: correct for exposure/selection bias by reweighting observations by the inverse of their probability of treatment assignment
when_to_use: observational data where treatment is not randomly assigned, correcting for selection bias in survival or usage models
when_not_to_use: randomized controlled trials (no bias to correct), extreme propensity scores near 0 or 1 (extreme weights destabilize)
governance_risks: positivity assumption violated with extreme propensity scores
model misspecification of propensity model propagates to weights
doubly robust methods preferred when possible
course_note: Evidenced in Collision_Severity_Framework_v2.docx — used for VMT-normalized severity rate estimation

what_it_solves: structured storage, querying, and analytical retrieval of business data through normalized relational tables
when_to_use: operational business data management, KPI computation, enterprise reporting, data foundation for analytics pipeline
when_not_to_use: unstructured data (use document stores), very high write throughput (use NoSQL), purely analytical OLAP (use columnar stores)
governance_risks: data access controls and row-level security
failure_modes: unbounded query without index causes timeout
course_note: MBAN 5550 — CruiseLine DB Project (instructor: Hai Wang); multi-table schema with DDL, Power Query ETL, Power BI visualization
